violations.text: The outside dumpster lids were not closed. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept closed. Dumpster lids were closed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is certified with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Application printed and left with the PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, cold held foods such as Milk and Yogurt were observed held at 46 and 48 degrees Fahrenheit respectively. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk and yogurt were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, the single door reach-in cooler unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 46 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures observed at 46 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, the unit was recently serviced. Use of the unit for cold holding of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until serviced/repaired/replaced. Foods to be held in colder refrigeration until the Single Door Reach-In Cooler Unit is properly holding ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, hot foods such as sausage and hot dogs were observed held at 117 and 115 degrees Fahrenheit respectively. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Delivery temperatures were reviewed. Food items were delivered less than one hour prior to inspection at or above 135 degrees Fahrenheit. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, cold foods such as milk and yogurt were observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Refrigeration unit was set to a colder setting at the time of inspection. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, the single door reach-in cooler was observed holding an ambient temperature of 44 to 52 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was adjusted to colder temperature setting at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024
